title: Trans-Australian Railway - Wikipedia
content:
| Feature | Metric Units | Imperial Units |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Longest Straight Section** | 478.193 kilometres | 297.135 miles (also cited as 297 miles) |
| **Total Line Length (Intro)** | 1691 kilometres | 1051 miles |
| **Total Line Length (As Constructed)** | 1692.60 kilometres | 1051.73 miles |
| **Total Line Length (1909 Survey)** | 1711 kilometres | 1,063 miles |

### Endpoints of the Straight Section
*   **Western End:** Located 793 miles (1,276 km) from Port Augusta, between Loongana and Nurina.
*   **Eastern End:** Terminates at the 496 miles [sic] point between Ooldea and Watson.

### Survey Details
*   **1907:** Legislation passed allowing for the route to be surveyed.
*   **1909:** Survey completed.
*   **Survey Findings:** Endorsed a route from Port Augusta via Tarcoola to Kalgoorlie.
